name: Sysmon service state changed
definition: Sysmon Event ID 4 reports that the Sysmon service state changed.
trigger_scenarios: Microsoft documents that this event reports the Sysmon service state as started or stopped.

key_fields.explanation: The service state transition. A stopped state means Sysmon telemetry collection may be degraded or absent.
UTC timestamp for aligning the service transition with process creation, service-control, and configuration-change events.
The host whose Sysmon service state changed; prioritize domain controllers, jump hosts, and security tooling servers.

false_positives: Approved Sysmon upgrades and configuration reloads can restart the service.
Host shutdown and boot cycles can produce expected started or stopped transitions.

attck_mapping.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools

detection_notes: Use threshold=1 unexpected stopped transition on high-value hosts. Microsoft documents Event 4 as Sysmon service started or stopped telemetry; a stopped state after sysmon64.exe -u, sc.exe stop, or a service-control process under C:\Windows\System32 can indicate T1562.001 Disable or Modify Tools. Correlate UtcTime with Event 1 process creation, Event 16 configuration changes, and Event 255 errors before suppressing planned upgrades.
kql_snippet:
Sysmon
| where EventID == 4
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
sample_log:
UtcTime: 2026-07-13 03:00:00.000
State: Stopped
Image: C:\Windows\System32\sc.exe
Computer: HOST01

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 4
queries.query:
Event
| where EventLog == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" and EventID == 4
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, EventID, RenderedDescription
| order by TimeGenerated desc
assumptions: The Sysmon Operational channel is collected into Event.
Map raw XML fields to the event-specific fields described on this page.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 4
queries.query:
index=sysmon source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=4
| table _time, host, EventCode, Message
assumptions: Index, source, and parsed field names vary by deployment.
Apply the documented field conditions after validating local field extraction.

queries.title: Retrieve Sysmon Event ID 4
assumptions: Confirm Provider is Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon because event IDs are not globally unique.
Inspect raw provider fields because parsed names vary by collection path.

queries.title: Sysmon Event ID 4
queries.query:
title: Sysmon Event ID 4
status: experimental
logsource:
    product: windows
    service: sysmon
detection:
    selection:
        EventID: 4
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative or system activity
level: informational
assumptions: This rule retrieves the event and is not a standalone malicious-behavior detection.
Add the event-specific conditions and correlations documented on this page.

value_references.meaning: Sysmon service state changed; this event cannot be filtered.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret EventID with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

value_references.meaning: The Sysmon service reported a stopped transition.
value_references.security_relevance: Interpret State with the other documented fields and correlated events; this value alone is not proof of compromise.

faqs.question: What does Sysmon Event ID 4 mean?
faqs.answer: The Sysmon service started or stopped.

faqs.question: Does Event ID 4 always indicate malicious activity?
faqs.answer: No. Reboots, upgrades, and approved maintenance produce state changes.

faqs.question: How should Event ID 4 be investigated?
faqs.answer: Review State and UtcTime and correlate with process creation, configuration changes, and service errors.

sources.title: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools (T1562.001)
